description: Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a medium and small campus deployment with Underlay RP placement at any VTEP or Aggregate with PIM-BIDIR, traffic loss will be observed due to a bud node scenario.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what caveat is documented for VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl?

Feature Caveat: VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl - Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a medium and small campus deployment with Underlay RP placement at any VTEP or Aggregate with PIM-BIDIR, traffic loss will be observed due to a bud node scenario.

description: Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a 3-clos data center design when there is link failure between the VTEP and SPINE, the Underlay Multicast routing path towards the RP picks up a Leaf VTEP as a Transit Router, which can cause traffic loss.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what limitation is mentioned for VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl?

Feature Caveat: VxLAN-Ulay- Mcast-Repl - Overlay flood and overlay multicast traffic loss may occur in underlay multicast VxLAN replication mode. In a 3-clos data center design when there is link failure between the VTEP and SPINE, the Underlay Multicast routing path towards the RP picks up a Leaf VTEP as a Transit Router, which can cause traffic loss.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: Kernel panic was observed while collecting the support files. Scenario: This issue is seen when multicast traffic passes through the switch and when memory consumption exceeds due to logs that got accumulated over a long period of time. Workaround: Disable and enable router PIM or restart the PIM daemon.

description: Symptom: The RPVST daemon crashes unexpectedly. Scenario: This issue is observed when an STP-aware switch is connected to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. Workaround: Avoid connecting STP-aware switches to spanning-tree guard enabled ports. Alternatively, remove the BPDU-guard configuration to temporarily avoid further crashes.
symptom: The RPVST daemon crashes unexpectedly.
scenario: This issue is observed when an STP-aware switch is connected to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured.
workaround: Avoid connecting STP-aware switches to spanning-tree guard enabled ports. Alternatively, remove the BPDU-guard configuration to temporarily avoid further crashes.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: The precision time protocol (PTP) offset is not accurate. Scenario: When configuring transceivers to a non-default forward error correction (FEC) setting, the PTP offset will not be accurate due to an inaccuracy in PTP latency. Workaround: Use the default FEC.
symptom: The precision time protocol (PTP) offset is not accurate.
scenario: When configuring transceivers to a non-default forward error correction (FEC) setting, the PTP offset will not be accurate due to an inaccuracy in PTP latency.
workaround: Use the default FEC.

description: Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.
symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up.
